sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check the current state after moving LEFT.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

print("Current nodes:")
for n in nodes:
    print(f"  Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Player position
green = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
white = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'W']
print(f"\nGreen: {green[0]['boundary'] if green else 'NOT FOUND'}")
print(f"White: {white[0]['boundary'] if white else 'NOT FOUND'}")

# Target frames
c_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'c' and n['pixels'] == 12]
print(f"\nTarget frames (c, 12px): {len(c_nodes)}")
for cn in c_nodes:
    print(f"  boundary={cn['boundary']}")

# Check for G frames
G_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'G' and n['pixels'] == 12]
print(f"G frames: {len(G_nodes)}")
for gn in G_nodes:
    print(f"  boundary={gn['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:

- Green: rows 24-27, cols 41-43 (moved from rows 25-27, cols 40-43 to rows 24-27, cols 41-43... wait, that's not a simple LEFT movement)
- White: rows 24-27, col 40 (a vertical strip to the LEFT of green)
- G frame: rows 24-27, cols 36-39 - THE TARGET ACTIVATED!
- The old G frame at rows 20-23, cols 40-43 changed back to 'c'
So the target at rows 24-27, cols 36-39 is now activated (changed to 'G')! The white block is at col 40, which is adjacent to the target at col 39. They share an edge.
Now I need to press SPACE to collect this target!
